info.title: CUSIP Data API
info.description:
A CUSIP is a nine-character numeric or alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a financial security for the purposes of facilitating clearing and settlement of trades. CUSIP is a registered trademark of the American Bankers Association. CUSIP Global Services (CGS) is managed on behalf of the American Bankers Association by FactSet Research Systems Inc. CGS identifiers cover a wide range of global financial instruments, including extensive equity and debt issues, derivatives, syndicated loans and U.S. / Canadian listed equity options and single stock futures.
Some useful links:
- [CUSIP](https://www.cusip.com/)
- [American Bankers Association](https://www.aba.com/)

get.summary: Search the CUSIP database using various filter parameters

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
The syntax is `:` pair separated by colon and multiple `:` pairs can be connected by AND, OR and NOT logical operator. #### Supported Search Fields | Search Field | Data Type | Example | Comment | |-------------- |-----------|------------ |-----| | cusip | string | cusip:023135106 || | isin | string | isin:US0231351067 |International Securities Identification Number (ISIN) is an alpha numeric code that contains 12 characters| | issuerName | string | issuerName:FactSet |Legal name of entity issuing securities| | issuerNumber | string | issuerNumber:023135 |The first six characters of a CUSIP identify the issuer of the security, whether a company, government agency or municipality.| | issuerGroup | string | issuerGroup:C |C for CUSIP, I for CINS and P for Private Placement Number (PPN)| | issuerType | string | issuerType:C |C for Corporate, M for Municipal and G for Government| | issuerState | string | issuerState:NJ |The two-letter code used to identify U.S. states| | issuerLogDate | date | issuerLogDate:[2023-10-29 TO 2023-11-30]
issuerLogDate:[2023-10-29 08:07:00 TO 2023-11-30 08:07:00] |Support both dates and timestamps.| | domicile | string | domicile:US |Two-character ISO domicile code; country of incorporation for an issuer| | currencyCode | string | currencyCode:USD |Three-character ISO currency code for a security.| | issueNumber | string | issueNumber:10 |The seventh and eighth characters of a CUSIP uniquely identify each issue from a given issuer.| | issueDescription | string |issueDescription:COM |Name of security being issued.| | issueType | string | issueType:E issueType:D | E for equity security, D for debt security| | issueGroup | string | issueGroup:V |C for CUSIP, I for CINS, P for Private Placement Number (PPN), V for private equity/credit | | rate |number | rate:[3.1 TO 3.4] |Coupon rate; the annual rate of interest payable on a security, expressed as a percentage.| | maturityDate | date |maturityDate:[2016-10-29 TO 2016-11-30]|The date the principal comes due and payable to the security holder.| | datedDate | date |datedDate:[2016-10-29 TO 2016-11-30] | The datedDate is also known as settlement date. It is the specific date from which interest begins to accrue on a fixed-income security| | issueLogDate | date |issueLogDate:[2016-10-29 TO 2016-11-30]
issueLogDate:[2016-10-29 08:07:00 TO 2016-11-30 18:07:00]|The date/time when CUSIP data is updated. Support both dates and timestamps.| | issueReleaseDate | date |issueReleaseDate:[2023-10-29 TO 2023-11-30]
issueReleaseDate:[2022-10-29 08:07:00 TO 2022-11-30 18:07:00]|The date/time when CUSIP data is created and released. Support both dates and timestamps.| | tickerSymbol |string |tickerSymbol:AMZN
tickerSymbol:(AMZN FDS MSFT) | Support multiple ticker symbols search. | | mic |string |mic:XNAS | ISO 10383 operating MIC code| | whereTraded |string |whereTraded:Nasdaq | Exchange name | | usCfiCode |string |usCfiCode:ESVUVR |Accept six-letter US CFI (Classification of Financial Instruments) code| | isoCfiCode |string |isoCfiCode:ESVUFR |Accept six-letter ISO CFI (Classification of Financial Instruments) code| | lei |string |lei:ZXTILKJKG63JELOEG630
lei:(ZXTILKJKG63JELOEG630 549300PCYWUCTLW37P50 2549009Y3WZIHLCUDO77) |The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-character, alpha-numeric code based on the ISO 17442 standard. Support multiple LEI search. | | section13f |string |section13f:Y | CUSIP as disclosed in the quarterly SEC Form 13F filing |

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Specifies the maximum number of CUSIP records to return in the response.

Accepts any integer between 1 and 10,000. Default is 10,000.

For trial users, the maximum number of records in the response is limited to 5

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
The page number in the search results.  Use this parameter to
navigate to paginated page.

Example: Any integer between 1 and 30.  Default:1

Note: If the maximum `pageSize` and `page` number are used, the API can return up to 300,000 records per CUSIP search.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Specifies the field and direction for sorting the API response. 
Format: `<sort field> <sort direction>` (e.g., "cusip asc" or "rate desc").
If the sort direction is not provided, ascending (`asc`) is used by default.

Supported sort fields:
  - cusip
  - isin
  - issuerName
  - issuerNumber
  - maturityDate
  - rate
  - issueLogDate
  - issueReleaseDate

parameters.name: fieldList
parameters.description:
Specify the response fields by providing a comma-separated list of field names.

Available fields:
  - cusip
  - isin
  - issuerNumber
  - issueNumber
  - issuerName
  - domicile
  - issuerGroup
  - issuerStatus
  - issuerType
  - issuerState
  - issuerLogDate
  - currencyCode
  - issueDescription
  - issueType
  - issueGroup
  - rate
  - maturityDate
  - datedDate
  - issueLogDate
  - issueReleaseDate
  - tickerSymbol
  - mic
  - whereTraded
  - usCfiCode
  - isoCfiCode
  - section13f

To return all CUSIP attributes, use: `fieldList=all`

200.description: Successful retrieval of CUSIP search result.

401.description: CUSIP search API call is unauthorized.

get.summary: Retrieve CUSIP data for a given CUSIP identifier

parameters.name: cusip
parameters.description: The 9-character CUSIP identifier to look up.

get.summary: Retrieve CUSIP data for the comma-delimited CUSIP identifiers

parameters.name: cusipList
parameters.description: Comma-delimited CUSIP identifiers.

parameters.name: cusip

get.summary: Search syndicated loan CUSIP data using various filter parameters

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
The syntax is `<search field>:<search value>` pair separated by colon and multiple `<search field>:<search value>` pairs can be connected by AND, OR and NOT logical operator.
#### Supported Search Fields
| Search Field   | Data Type | Example        | Comment |
|--------------  |-----------|------------  |-----|
| cusip          | string    | cusip:023135106         ||
| borrowerName   | string    | borrowerName:Bank         ||
| borrowerIsoDomicile | string    | borrowerIsoDomicile:US    ||
| borrowerStateCode    | string    | borrowerStateCode:DE         ||
| isoCurrency     | string    | isoCurrency:USD         ||
| maturityDate     | date    | maturityDate:[2022-09-02 TO 2028-09-02]         ||
| facilityLogDate     | date    | facilityLogDate:[2024-09-02 TO 2024-09-02]         ||

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Specifies the maximum number of CUSIP records to return in the response.

Accept any integer between 1 and 10000. Default:1000

For trial users, the maximum number of records in the response is limited to 5

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Specifies the field and direction for sorting the API response. 
Format: `<sort field> <sort direction>` (e.g., "cusip asc" or "borrowerName desc").
The default sort field is cusip. If the sort direction is not provided, ascending (`asc`) is used by default.

Supported sort fields:
  - cusip
  - borrowerName

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
The page number in the search result.  User uses this parameter to navigate to next page.

Example: Any integer between 1 and 10.  Default:1

*If user uses maximum pageSize and page number, API can return up to 100,000 records per API call

200.description: Successful retrieval of CUSIP Issuer result.

parameters.name: cusip_or_cusipList
parameters.description: Give a valid option or future CUSIP Identifier

get.summary: Search option and future CUSIP data using various filter parameters

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
The syntax is `<search field>:<search value>` pair separated by colon and multiple `<search field>:<search value>` pairs can be connected by AND, OR and NOT logical operator.
#### Supported Search Fields
| Search Field   | Data Type | Example        | Comment |
|--------------  |-----------|------------  |-----|
|  cusip          | string    | cusip:00E01FUX6         ||
|  isin          | string    | isin:US00E01FUX60         ||
|  underlyingCusip          | string    | underlyingCusip:00206R102         ||
|  underlyingIsin          | string    | underlyingIsin:US00206R1023         ||
|  contractLevelFoIndicator    | string    | contractLevelFoIndicator:C         | C = Call Option, P = Put Option, F = Futures Contract| 
|  occCode   | string    | occCode:"T     260116C00022000"         ||
| opraOsiCode   | string    | opraOsiCode:"T    A1626D220000"         ||
| underlyingSymbol | string    | underlyingSymbol:T    ||
| contractName    | string    | contractName:"AT&T Incorporated"         ||
| strikePrice     | string    | strikePrice:20         ||
| promptDate     | date    | promptDate:[2022-09-02 TO 2028-09-02]         ||

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Specifies the maximum number of CUSIP records to return in the response.

Accept any integer between 1 and 10000. Default:1000

For trial users, the maximum number of records in the response is limited to 5

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Specifies the field and direction for sorting the API response. 
Format: `<sort field> <sort direction>` (e.g., "optionFutureCusip asc" or "contractName desc").
The default sort field is cusip. If the sort direction is not provided, ascending (`asc`) is used by default.

Supported sort fields:
  - cusip
  - isin
  - underlyingCusip
  - underlyingIsin
  - occCode

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
The page number in the search result.  User uses this parameter to navigate to next page.

Example: Any integer between 1 and 10.  Default:1

*If user uses maximum pageSize and page number, API can return up to 100,000 records per API call

200.description: Successful retrieval of CUSIP Issuer result.

get.summary: Search CUSIP data using corporate action filters

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
The syntax is `:` pair separated by colon and multiple `:` pairs can be connected by AND, OR and NOT logical operator. #### Supported Search Fields | Search Field | Data Type | Example | Comment | |-------------- |-----------|------------ |-----| | type | string| type:"Name Change" |Corporate action type - "Name Change", "Reverse Split", "Reorganized", "Merged", "Issue Reorganized"| | issuerName | string| issuerName:Amazon |Issuer name | | effectiveDate | date | effectiveDate:[2023-10-29 TO 2023-11-30] | Corporate action effective date | | logDate | date | logDate:[2023-10-29 TO 2023-11-30]
logDate:[2023-10-29 08:07:00 TO 2023-10-29 10:07:00] | Support both day and timestamp |

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The max number of records in the response

Any integer between 1 and 10000. Default:10000

For Trial user: Max no of records in the response is 5

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
The page number in the search result.  User uses this parameter to navigate to next page.

Example: Any integer between 1 and 10.  Default:1

*If user uses maximum pageSize and page number, API can return up to 100,000 records per API call

The max number of records in the response

Any integer between 1 and 10000. Default:1000

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Specifies the field and direction for sorting the API response. 
Format: `<sort field> <sort direction>` (e.g., "cusip asc" or "projectId desc").
The default sort field is cusip. If the sort direction is not provided, ascending (`asc`) is used by default.

Supported sort fields:

Example: Any integer between 1 and 10.  Default:1

lei.description: 20-character alphanumeric code based on the ISO 17442 standard that uniquely identifies distinct legal entities

maturityDate.description: The maturity date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

datedDate.description: The dated date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

firstCouponDate.description: The first coupon date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

closingDate.description: The closing date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

effectiveDate.description: The effective date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

logDate.description: The log date in MM/DD/YYYY HH:mm:ss format.

borrowerName.example: JPMORGAN CHASE BK N A - SYNDICATED LNS

dealAdl.example: CORRECT BORROWER NAME IS JPMORGAN CHASE BANK NA COMMINGLED PENSION TR FDS

creditAgreementDate.description: The credit agreement date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

maturityDate.description: The maturity date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

promptDate.description: The prompt date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

contractLevelFoIndicator.description: C = Call Option, P = Put Option, F = Futures Contract

contractStartDate.description: The contract start date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

primaryFlag.description: Indicates whether this is the primary ticker. "1" denotes primary, "0" denotes not primary.

projectName.example: The Nature Conservancy Washington Rainforest Renewal Project

state.description: Country state the project is active in, if available

projectStartDate.description: The project start date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

developerLei.description: The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-character, alpha-numeric code based on the ISO 17442 standard

methodology.example: Improved Forest Management (IFM) on Non‐Federal U.S. Forestlands

creditingPeriodStartDate.description: The crediting period start date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

creditingPeriodEndDate.description: The crediting period end date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

firstCreditIssuanceDate.description: The first credit issuance date in MM/DD/YYYY format.

totalCredit.description: Total number of credits issued by the project for that vintage

outstandingCredit.description: Total number of credits issued by the project minus total number of credits retired for that project and the current vintage.

addDate.description: The add date in MM/DD/YYYY HH:mm:ss format.

updateDate.description: The update date in MM/DD/YYYY HH:mm:ss format.

tags.name: CUSIP
tags.description: CUSIPs are 9-character identifiers that capture an issue’s important differentiating characteristics for issuers and their financial instruments in the U.S. and Canada.
